programme.title: Walkers Unpacked: Inside the Crisp Factory
programme.desc: Its crunch time, as one of Britains best-known brands launches its latest crisp flavour. From soil to shelf, we tear into the journey of how Walkers new crisp is born.

programme.title: Jamies Feasts for a Fiver
programme.desc: Jamie makes gnarly celeriac skewers, a hearty spiced cauliflower bake complete with an edible lid, a steaming winter chilli, crispy potato and chickpea parcels and soul-warming soup.

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours in...
programme.desc: Rose Matafeo joins Joe for some mischief in Malmo, as they visit the worlds first twisting tower and the Disgusting Food Museum,and sample a selection of insects and a spirit made from beaver glands.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: A look back at one of Kirstie and Phils biggest builds, when one family went all in to overhaul their Huddersfield home beyond recognition. Paul and Dawns higgledy-piggledy layout didnt work for them anymore, so Kirstie got the sledgehammer out.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hosting in Reading, fun-loving Sonya Smith knows her cooking skills arent the best and hopes to bungle her way through the night, and win the guests over, with her good humour and Touch of Sparkle theme. There are kitchen calamities galore, and Sonya quite literally wets herself laughing. Grumpy geezer Glen is unimpressed with the choice of entertainment, curvaceous corset-maker Serine teaches some decorum.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cocky Cockney Glen Wilkie serves up a menu he is convinced will be a winner. Even a disaster with his `super duper tiramisu doesnt dent his confidence. His main of Bambi on a plate causes tears at the table from Sonya Smith and a refusal from Sabi Phagura. Sensitive student Richard Howse can only sit by and watch with his new best friend Serine Daou as the night descends into chaos.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the third night in Reading and the turn of mature student Richard Howse. With a menu and set of instructions from his omnipresent girlfriend, Katy, Richard sets about making his pirate themed night the best of the week. A treasure hunt brings out Glen Wilkies true feelings about fellow guest Serine Daou, and Sonya Smith takes a disliking to some of the gluten-free fare on offer.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness fanatic Sabi Phagura goes back to her roots with a bhangra-themed evening that puts her healthy lifestyle on the backburner for one day as she cooks traditional Indian food for her four guests. The food doesnt quite live up to everyones expectations, and her entertainment leaves Richard and Glen in an intimate situation. Now that his night is out of the way, Glen does everything he can to wind up everyone.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final evening in Reading, corset-maker Serine Daou has the daunting task of trying to end the week with a bang. Her gourmet burgers are more burger than gourmet, but - as part of her burlesque night - her guests costumes dont disappoint. When her entertainment doesnt appeal to Glen, he swings back into action with some more wind-ups.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Joanne and Nick are in a standoff over their bursting bungalow in Scorton. Can Kirstie turn compact into impact, or will an awe-inspiring Spencer special triumph?

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George Clarke explores more inspirational and ingenious amazing spaces, including a stargazing cabin with a twist, a vintage horsebox and a Norwegian Viking-inspired longhouse.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In south London, Kirstie and Phil help Jess and Zac, whove viewed over 60 properties to no avail, and then help self-professed oldest first-time buyers in town, Rachel and Alan.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul dreams of owning a holiday home on Spains Costa Blanca, to escape the winter months and spend quality time with family. Ben Hillman shows him what £80k can buy, but are any the one?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is house hunting around the beautiful hill-top town of Competa, Andalucía, with Michael and Seamus from Northern Ireland, who want a holiday home where they can entertain.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Annette and Graham explore Spains northern Costa Blanca to find their dream bolthole for £220k. After a rocky start, is the grass really greener? And will they find their perfect place?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Veronica and Tim search for a bolthole on the stunning Italian Riviera for £220k. After a few tantalising treats give them food for thought, will they find their slice of la dolce vita?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sarah sees what the Canary Island of Fuerteventura has to offer for £250k, from a beautiful box-ticking bungalow to a sensational semi-detached house. But will she find the perfect place?

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Its a Cullercoats catch-up, as we see how Mark and Suze are getting on after transforming their topsy-turvy townhouse. Do they still have the renovation bug?

programme.title: Fukushima: Days That Shocked the World
programme.desc: In March 2011, Japan was hit by a devastating tsunami killing 20,000 and triggering a nuclear meltdown. Survivors and witnesses tell their extraordinary stories.

programme.title: Fukushima: Days That Shocked the World
programme.desc: In March 2011, a massive tsunami caused a major meltdown at Japans Fukushima nuclear plant, triggering explosions and evacuations. What went wrong at Fukushima?

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Bewilderness
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Bill Bailey performs live at the Grand Theatre in Swansea and delivers hilariously unfocused and scattered comedy styles, as well as his enviable musical skills.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jenno and Bucko race to assist when Jim is rammed by a runaway driver, and a have-a-go hero helps to tackle a runaway drug driver.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Sean uses the big red key to give a drug dealer a door knock he wont forget, and Jenno gets a face full of pepper spray.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The police interceptors tackle a difficult night shift. Ortis is with Spike as they pursue a fleeing car that drives into a dead end, while Rick is with dog handler Ian as theyre called to back up the firearms squad.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Americas Speeders
programme.desc: In the middle of New Jerseys car theft epidemic, Patrolmen Maurice Marsilla and Bill Gibson pull over a suspicious Lamborghini that is 1000 miles from home; they discover that the driver is a wanted man but he doesnt want to stick around.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: PCs Ian and Matt pull over a Chevvy. A search of the vehicle reveals drugs paraphernalia. In Warrington, cops the hunt for an uninsured driver who may be drunk.

programme.title: Banged up Abroad
programme.desc: After days embedded with rebels from the Free Syrian Army, rookie photographer Matthew Schrier decides hes ridden his luck long enough. But on his way out of Aleppo hes kidnapped by Al Nusra Front, the Syrian Al Qaeda.

programme.title: Banged up Abroad
programme.desc: In 2012 Simone Blignaut has a lucrative job working for one of Johannesburgs biggest drug dealers, but when her boss entrusts her to fly to Bolivia and secure a major cocaine deal, the 19-year-old Simone is in over her head.

programme.title: Banged up Abroad

programme.sub-title: The Mary Rose: Secrets From the Deep
programme.desc: The Mary Rose, Henry VIIIs great warship, went down nearly five centuries ago, killing more than 400 men. A host of theories have emerged to explain the disaster, including enemy fire, the ships builders, an unruly crew, and even the King himself.

programme.title: Great British Ships
programme.desc: Rob Bell reveals the story of the SS Great Britain. When constructed it became the longest passenger ship in the world. Its early years were beset with trouble, but it later found success carrying immigrants to Australia.

programme.title: Great British Ships
programme.desc: HMS Victory was already 40 years old before it secured its place in history at Trafalgar. Rob discovers the extraordinary feats of engineering behind its construction, and the celebrity of its famous captain, Nelson.

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: With election day close at hand, Nelson Hayward finds himself in an extremely tight race for a Senate seat from California, but when a political campaign manager is murdered, Lt Columbo starts trailing the senatorial candidate and uncovers some nasty facts.

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: Columbo suspects that a soap opera star, who plays an egotistical detective, has killed is former lover.

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: On holiday in a normally peaceful Mexican resort, Lt Columbo finds his rest disrupted by another unusual murder involving the world of bullfighting. When his ever-unreliable car breaks down, the dishevelled detective teams up with a local policeman.

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: Elderly and wealthy Clifford Paris is about to be married to the much younger Lisa Chalmers, a fellow physical fitness enthusiast. Unhappy about the impending nuptials, his nephew, Dexter, the host of a television cooking show, watches as Clifford fences with the family attorney Michael Hathaway. After a vigorous match, Clifford decides to take a bath. Dexter approaches his soaking uncle with an unexpected wedding present.

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: A wealthy psychiatrist murders the husband of his mistress to protect his own reputation.

programme.title: When TV Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Memorable mishaps include a premature attraction on Take Me Out, and Dale Winton and Barbara Windsor filling time when a National Lottery stunt fails.

programme.title: Taggart

programme.title: Unwrapping King Tut: Secrets of the Mummy
programme.desc: One hundred years ago the world celebrated one of historys most famous archaeological moments, when Howard Carter finally unwrapped the mummy of Tutankhamun. Dr Xand van Tulleken sets out to reexamine the six days it took to reveal the Boy King.

programme.title: Mysteries Of The Pyramids with Dara OBriain
programme.desc: With the Royal Pyramids of Egypt as a backdrop, Dara O? Briain, assisted by Egyptologist Chris Naunton and archaeologist Raksha Dave, delves into why and how were these pyramids built.

programme.desc: John visits a couple in Gunner, near Salisbury, who wanted to build a garage next to their bungalow but did not realise they were living by a fifth-century graveyard. The programme follows a team of archaeologists as they excavate off the skeleton of a 30-year-old man, attempting to resolve whether he was a Saxon or a native Briton.

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents an edition of the old-time music hall programme, from the stage of the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds. With Hylda Baker, Val Doonican, Richard Hearne, Robin Hunter and members of the Players Theatre, London.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Its the 50th anniversary of the Blainthorp Music Festival and there is fierce competition to be chosen as Golden Voice. Suddenly, the finalists start losing their voices and Hetty must find out why.

programme.title: The Turkish Detective
programme.desc: Detective Mehmet Suleyman arrives in Istanbul to join the formidable Inspector Cetin Ikmens homicide unit, alongside Detective Ayse Farsakoglu and IT guru Tarik, and finds himself embroiled in his first murder investigation.

programme.title: The Turkish Detective
programme.desc: With the new development on Gozdes case, suspicion initially falls on her secret boyfriend Kemal. However, after Kemals interrogation, and the non-confirmation of another mans alibi, a new suspect is formed.

programme.title: Wogan
programme.sub-title: Jenny Agutter, Laurie Lee, John Garnett
programme.desc: Terry Wogans guests are Jenny Agutter, Laurie Lee, who recites a piece from As I Walked Out One Midsummer Morning, and John Garnett. Barbara Dickson sings The Right Moment.

programme.title: Comic Roots
programme.desc: Kenneth Williams explores his roots in and around Londons St Pancras, where he grew up. Architecture, poetry, art and music were the formative influences on Kenneth Williams: the slum architecture of St Pancras where he grew up; the liquid poetry of his grans fruity anecdotes; the art of the Marcel wave practised by his hairdresser father; and the musical knees-up of `The Boot.

programme.title: Wogan
programme.sub-title: Kenneth Williams, Barbara Windsor, Stephen Fry, Michael Palin
programme.desc: Kenneth Williams stands in for Terry Wogan, chatting with guests Barbara Windsor and husband Steve Hollings, Stephen Fry and Michael Palin. Music by Hank Marvin and The Shadows.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Its the 50th anniversary of the Blainthorp Music Festival and there is fierce competition to be chosen as Golden Voice. Suddenly, the finalists start losing their voices and Hetty must find out why.

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents an edition of the old-time music hall programme, from the stage of the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds. With Hylda Baker, Val Doonican, Richard Hearne, Robin Hunter and members of the Players Theatre, London.

The programme follows a team of archaeologists as they excavate off the skeleton of a 30-year-old man, attempting to resolve whether he was a Saxon or a native Briton.

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys

programme.desc: The guys tackle some tough talking competition on Tons home turf, Antelope Valley, California. They pick up two cheap units, but will there be anything of value inside?

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: Ton, Allen and their feisty new assistant find a pair of high-tech gadgets and a vintage sled at a lively auction in Honolulu. Can they sell these unusual items for a profit?

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: The guys put their bidding skills to the test against the Hawaiian locals. They discover a vintage motor scooter and some antique Polynesian tattoo tools. What are they worth?

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: Ton, Allen and Carolyn find a giant remote control car at a shipping container auction in L.A. Can they tempt a collector to part with his cash in return for this rare off-roader?

programme.title: Whats New Scooby-Doo?
programme.desc: The gang investigate claims that the ghost of a magician is haunting the concert hall where their friend is playing.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob visits his old pal Bubble Buddys hometown which is called Bubbletown.

programme.title: Baby Jake
programme.sub-title: Baby Jake Loves to Say Hello
programme.desc: Baby Jake and Nibbles the Rabbit go on an orchard adventure and enjoy saying hello.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch tries to teach the Grimbots by drawing on a chalkboard but his last piece of chalk snaps. Meanwhile, the Go Jetters arrive at the White Cliffs of Dover in England to cheer on Kyan as he finishes a cross-channel swim.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bings friends are coming to his house for a cake party. Sula arrives first and Bing cant wait to show her the cake hes made, but he accidentally tips the cake off its stand and breaks it into pieces. Bing is upset that the cake is ruined but Amma helps him see it differently.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster is ready to party on Party All Day Day. Elder Chick is the MC for the day and says that she is loving Love Monsters party spirit, but she warns him to make sure he saves some energy for the grand finale conga at the end of the day.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo needs to deliver a pizza to a very hard-to-reach island, but some stubborn seabirds have their eye on the pizza. Will Mojo have to go supersonic to get past them?

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: When Brewsters hero cant make it for Hero Day, he and Zephie set off on their own hero duty and learn what makes a real hero along the way.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: To plug an air vent at the bottom of the lake, Ginger and the baby Pea-Rexes must seek the help of the mighty Watermelonsuchus.

programme.title: My Petsaurus
programme.desc: Chloe is struggling to teach Topsy how to beg, but an ice cream break is all the inspiration Topsy needs to learn.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its the exceptional painter Spinach creating Kiwis portrait, if Kiwi can keep still.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Follow the adventures of Supertato, the worlds first potato superhero as he tackles the mischievous Evil Pea, in the supermarket aisles.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are playing a vooshing game with their favourite toys, Hoppity Voosh and Hippo Fairy. They throw them into the air to see how high they can fly, but Sulas hippo gets stuck in a tree. Bing throws Hoppity up to save her, but Hoppity gets stuck too.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Coco want to read Charlie a story. Bing wants to read Biteysaurus Big Book of Dinosaurs, and Coco wants to read The Rainbow Fairy Mice and the Disappointed Dragon. Bings book is first, but when Coco tries to bring the Rainbow Fairy Mice into the story, Bing is frustrated, thats not how it goes! But Flop shows Bing how they can combine the two tales together.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are close to finishing a jigsaw puzzle when the Naughty Mice steal their last piece. To get it back they must take part in a series of puzzles in an underground adventure set by the Puzzler.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Roly has brought his big brothers action hero figure to the Clubhouse. His name is Jet Rocket. He is known for zooming through space, smashing and crashing things and most importantly, saving the world.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Its Go Jetters glow when Glitch uses some glow-worms for his disco pizzazz in New Zealand.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Big Move
programme.desc: When Lilys father decides to move the family out of the Lake District, Peter comes up with a clever but dangerous plan to change Dr Bobtails mind.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Captain Barnacles and his intrepid band of explorers roam the oceans in search of adventure and fun. When Peso and the Octonauts aid an enormous Elephant Seal, he overstays his welcome in the Octopod.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo only wanted to help fix a satellite dish with Mojos Chopper Top. Now theyve been mistaken for a UFO, and the whole town is in a tizzy about invading aliens!

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Sophie must infiltrate the sports hall without disturbing a relaxation class Val is running to retrieve bibs, goals and a big bag of footballs.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.sub-title: Were All Friends: Time for Bed
programme.desc: Mr Tumble, Maddie and friends get ready to say good night.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.sub-title: Bumpy and the Wise Old Wolfhound
programme.desc: Bluey sends a video to Bingo in hospital to show her that everyone gets sick sometimes.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey is unable to get to sleep, so Mum reads her one last story. Unicorse, the most annoying hand puppet in the world, interrupts them, and Bluey tries to reform it.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Tag is feeling grumpy because he didnt eat his breakfast. Luckily, Duggee has his Breakfast Badge, so he can make whatever the Squirrels would like to eat - and it turns out they all like very different things for breakfast! The Squirrels learn all about how different animals fuel themselves in the mornings, earning their Breakfast Badges in the process. Tag finally gets his toast, helping power him through the day. Well done Squirrels!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is putting up a picture of himself and the old friends he grew up with, who are just like family to him. This inspires the Squirrels to take a photo of their own, so they take turns getting smartened up for the camera with varying degrees of success. After a number of experimental poses, they eventually settle on the perfect composition, until Tag spots Enid - shes part of the family too, so shouldnt she be in the photo?

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Flutter buys a new fishy pet from slippery salesman Mervyn, only for the Piripenguins to get stuck in its gloopy slime. Can the friends find a solution to their sticky situation?

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Big Lizard isnt happy in his skin! Cosima tries everything to stop him scratching until she realises thats exactly what he needs to do. Hes shedding his skin!

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch is excited when Lars refits the Grimbler with new, clean biogas boosters that could make it even faster than the Vroomster! The boosters run on gas made from food waste, and luckily there is plenty of leftover food in the academy canteen for the Grimbots to gather up. But Glitch overloads the boosters and BOOM! He loses control of the Grimbler and is sent flying across the academy, out of control.

programme.title: Spidey and His Amazing Friends
programme.sub-title: Go Dino-Webs Go!; Ghost in the Museum
programme.desc: With the help of Reptil, Team Spidey gets new dino powers to defeat Gobbys powerful new Dino-Mech. Team Spidey must stop a ghost thats stealing valuable artefacts from the museum.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: When Paani goes missing in the desert, Dashi calls for back-up before he runs out of water.

programme.title: Stan Can
programme.desc: Butterflies on Stanleys solar panels mean he has no power for his vehicles or his Can-Bots!

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Follow the adventures of Supertato, the worlds first potato superhero as he tackles the mischievous Evil Pea, in the supermarket aisles.

programme.title: Talking Tom Heroes: Suddenly Super
programme.desc: The Heroes find themselves battling a rogue plant.

programme.title: Andys Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy travels back in time 65 million years to North America in the time of Tyrannosaurus Rex. His mission; to get a clay imprint of the skin of a T-rex.

programme.title: Big Cook Little Cook
programme.desc: Jen is being an artist today and trying to paint a cheetah, but she has run out of paint for the spots. They are visited by Ada the Artist and cook her a delicious arty pancake.

programme.title: Ranger Hamzas Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how allotments play an important role in the environment. They find out why they are great places to grow fruit and vegetables, explore a compost heap where worms and birds live and spot a water butt which recycles rainwater. On the way, they enjoy the peace and quiet of the allotment, before planting some potatoes.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Cheetah and Warthog
programme.desc: To meet some beautiful cheetahs, Aneeshwar and Lizzie lay down herbs and spices on logs in their enclosure. The two brothers love to find things with a strong odour. Aneeshwar and Lizzie see how the cheetahs use their sense of smell to achieve it.

programme.title: Waffles After School Club
programme.desc: Loud disco music disrupts Waffles After School Club experience until George the cat finds a solution for Waffle - dog earmuffs to protect his ears. Time for a doggy disco!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee shows the squirrels how to make a paper boat out of his newspaper. An army of ants need help to get across to the other side of the pond so they can join a picnic. Happy comes up with a very good idea - to make the ants a big paper boat.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: A poisonous sea krait lays her eggs in the garden pod, just as Captain Barnacles has to navigate the ship past a treacherous whirlpool.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Zigzags friend Banjo the koala doesnt feel like coming out to play, she calls Vida, who knows just what to do. Vida gets him exercising and healthy enough to play with Zigzag in no time!

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When Grandmaster Glitch builds his effigy in ice, his digging threatens the Lambert Glacier in Antarctica.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Its Rafis seventh birthday, and in her family of Wishing Wizards, that means shes finally old enough to receive her very own wishing wand. With her dads, Dash and Jake, Rafi learns the three important rules of wishing: you can only make one wish a day, wishes always run out and you cant unwish a wish. As Rafi holds her wand for the first time, she realises the possibilities are endless - and a little bit magical. Rafis birthday marks the beginning of a whole new chapter.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi loves bouncing on the trampoline and wishes she could keep bouncing all day. Her feet become bouncy like trampolines, and at first it feels brilliant. But bouncing everywhere makes simple jobs tricky - cups topple over, eggs splatting, and even hugs are hard. Rafi doesnt want help, but soon she sees that bouncing all the time isnt as fun as she thought. With her dads, she realises its okay to ask for help. Together they share pancakes, laughter, and a big upside-down hug.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: When a touring musical pigeon lands in the garden, the poor bird finds he has lost his flying beat. Yukee and the garden gang help Feathers find the right wing-flapping beat to get back in the air.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its the Peas performing their hap-pea double act and showing that, although they look alike, they definitely dont think alike.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends go on a fruit picking adventure. Meanwhile, Aunt Polly judges the annual Fabulous Fruit Competition, but what has Mr Tumble done to Grandads strawberries?

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: Gemma, Benjee Bear and Bailee Bear welcome toddlers Elena, Brodhi, Cavalli and Betsy, and their grown-ups, to the Toddler Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri and Lou decide to take Pania a giant fruit for a present. But as they bring it to her, they cant resist having a taste of it, and another, and another.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Impatient Kitty tries to build a machine alone, which leads to chaos.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Cocker Spaniel Diesel is a hearing dog for his teenage owner, Rayane, who is deaf. This means he doesnt hear everything. Diesels super ears are trained to alert Rayane to important sounds he might miss. Diesel is Rayanes ears! Diesel has to ignore lots of noises on the way to the pet shop. He uses his superhearing to zoom in on the important sounds like the pet shop door bell.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee cant stop playing his favourite game on Mos tablet. How is Mo going to convince him that its just as much fun playing outside?

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Catchers land on planet Cartoon and make friends with two cute alien kids: Toby and Penny. Their favourite game is Shoot the Can.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee plays I spy in a beautiful forest with her new friend Raffaella.

programme.title: Dylans Playtime Adventures
programme.desc: Sea captain Dylan wants to be the best captain ever, but he cannot be one without his crew.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo need to move a crashed meteor. But every time Mojo goes near the big rock, moving turns to grooving. How can you move a meteor if you cant stop dancing?

programme.title: Stan Can
programme.desc: Stanley helps Hattie, Fred, and Fifi with a new food delivery service. It starts well, except with each delivery a problem arises. Stanley must solve them before Myrtles party.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Supertato runs in fright when Pumpkin tries to make friends with him, but he later realises she needs his help as shes stuck in the air hanging from balloons set up by Evil Pea.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Brinicle turns news anchor and invents stories to wow his audience until things go wrong.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Ubercorn and Glitch head to the Svalbard Global Seed Vault, hoping to keep some seeds from Glitchs rare fruit tree safe for the future. But, just like when they were cadets together, the two of them get into trouble. Glitch accidentally crashes the Grimbler into the vault, and its hot engines start melting the ice that keeps the seeds cool. With the Go Jetters delayed by a malfunctioning Jet Pad, Ubercorn and Glitch try to save the scientists, the vault and Glitchs rare seeds.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: To celebrate the 400-year legacy of William Shakespeare, Donna and Simon head to ancient Verona and discover the story of `Romeo and Juliet.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Junos best friend is a baby bird and they talk all day, but when anyone else talks to him, he freezes up. Vida figures out the problem-hes shy-and gives the baby bird some tools to help him cope.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.sub-title: A Big Day Out for D.O.R.I.S.
programme.desc: Dad gets trapped in the shower when D.O.R.I.S. decides to visit the sea. Can Big Lizard and Cosima stop her before she does permanent damage?

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: As Mum and Dad struggle to put together their new flat-pack patio swing seat, Bluey and Bingo play a game and use the cardboard and bubble wrap to construct their own mini-civilisation.

programme.title: Nikhil and Jay
programme.sub-title: Nikhil and Jay and the Portrait
programme.desc: Nikhil has never painted a portrait of Jay before because he cant stay still! After trying lots of ways to keep his brother still, Nikhil finds the perfect solution.

programme.title: JoJo and Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: Winter: Its Time for a Community Newsletter
programme.desc: Great Gran Gran shows JoJo her St Lucian local newsletter, and JoJo is inspired to make her own community newsletter. They go out and about finding news for it. Cynthia tells them that a mystery guest is turning on the winter market lights, Charlene shares that she has special bunting for sale at the winter market, and Navid tells them about his new food stall. JoJo and Gran Gran get working on making their newsletter.

programme.title: Sarah and Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Ladys jukebox inspires a knitted instrument band.

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.sub-title: I Like My Hair Completely the Way It Is
programme.desc: Something is bothering Lola when its time to get a hair cut.

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Moon Baby, Pepi Nana and all the friends find a toy roundabout has been left in the Toy House. Each chooses a vehicle to ride in and they have a race. Mr Onion thinks his very slow Bumparoller will be last, but hes in for a surprise.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa look after Baby Tweety on a play date and discover it can be tiring work.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.sub-title: The Tombliboos Busy Ninky Nonk Day
programme.desc: The Tombliboos ride on the Ninky Nonk. The Ninky Nonk stops in a very lovely part of the garden and Tombliboos get off to chase after the ball. Then the Ninky Nonk sets off without the Tombliboos.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Brave the storm with Chris Hemsworth, as he reads a bedtime story about a little bear who overcomes his fear of thunder.

programme.sub-title: The One Where the Stripper Cries

Meanwhile, Monica and Rachel hire an ungainly stripper, Roy - without seeing him first - for Phoebes bachelorette party. Also, Chandler and Ross attend a college reunion and meet an attractive woman they both promised not to date during school.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Its Phoebe and Mikes big day, but all does not go to plan. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel makes the mistake of going for a job interview at a restaurant where her current boss is also dining. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Rachel makes a startling announcement, Ross secretly schemes to get her former job back for her. programme.title: Friends

Exasperated, Phoebe takes the bowl of everyones tickets and threatens to throw it off the balcony. The others make up, but a pigeon startles Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: A visit to the set of a soap opera inspires Rachel to dream of a romantic encounter with Joey, and Chandler must take Ross on a weekend getaway after Monica is unable to take time off from work.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Soap Opera Party
programme.desc: Ross is excited when he meets a stunning palaeontology professor, but when he takes her to Joeys rooftop party for the actors soap-opera co-stars, she intimidates him with her impressive list of former boyfriends. Meanwhile, Rachel and an intoxicated Monica make fools of themselves at the party chasing after the hunky daytime actors.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Fertility Test
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler have an embarrassing encounter with an old acquaintance at a fertility clinic, Ross plays Cyrano to help Joey woo a professor, and Rachel sneaks into a spa for a massage hoping Phoebe wont find out.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Disappointed to learn that they cannot conceive a baby naturally, Monica and Chandler consider their options. When Chandler invites a handsome colleague round for dinner, Monica and Chandler embarrassingly interview him as a potential sperm donor. Meanwhile, while shopping with Phoebe, Rachel admits that she still has a thing for Joey.

programme.title: Jumanji
programme.desc: Alan Parrish disappeared as a young boy. Nobody believes his friends story that he was sucked into the game they were playing. Twenty-six years later, two children find the game in their attic, and when they start to play, Alan is released, but the game isnt over yet, and Alan must finish before he is really free.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang heads to Barbados for a palaeontology convention where Ross is the keynote speaker. Rachel harbours steamy thoughts about Joey while David prepares to propose to Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After Joey and Rachel Kiss
programme.desc: While the friends are still in Barbados, Joey tries to tell Ross about his feelings for Rachel and Ross wants to confess to kissing Joeys girlfriend Charlie, but it is easier said than done. Meanwhile, Monica, Chandler and Phoebe can hear their amorous conversations through the hotels paper-thin walls. Also, Monicas hair problems go from bad to worse, and Phoebe has some bad news for Mikes girlfriend on her birthday.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Is Fine
programme.desc: Ross drinks too much wine while dining with Rachel, Joey and Charlie. Elsewhere, Frank Jr cannot cope with his precocious triplets and pays Phoebe a visit.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel and Joey nervously anticipate their first real date, Monica and Phoebe try to avoid an obnoxious friend with a fake British accent, and Ross emerges from a tanning booth with a two-toned appearance.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel gets Monica and Chandler to delay their long-awaited trip to Vermont for Emmas first birthday bash, but problems ensue when Emma falls asleep and a racy cake turns up.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachels Sister Baby-Sits
programme.desc: Rachel regrets trusting her self-indulgent little sister Amy with Emma, Monica regrets asking Joey to write a recommendation letter to the child adoption agency for her, and Phoebe accidentally thwarts the anniversary surprise Mike has in store for her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross applies for a palaeontology grant, but discovers that Dr Hobart - who allocates such funding - used to go out with his girlfriend Charlie. Meanwhile, Mike wants Phoebe to get rid of one of her bizarre paintings, but neither Rachel nor Monica is keen to take it, and Chandler lies to Joey about watching his audition tape.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Home Study
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler fuss over their apartment as they expect a visit from the adoption worker. Meanwhile, Phoebe and Mike opt for a small wedding and donate the extra money to a childrens charity.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Late Thanksgiving
programme.desc: Monica reluctantly agrees to cook Thanksgiving dinner, but locks everyone out when they turn up late as Joey and Ross went to a hockey game while Rachel and Phoebe took Emma to a baby beauty contest. Monica also receives some adoption news.

programme.title: Shanghai Knights
programme.desc: Anachronistic sequel to `Shanghai Noon. Two friends travel to 19th-century London to take back the Imperial Seal of China, which has been stolen by an evil English lord. Along the way they meet Jack the Ripper, Sir Arthur Conan Doyle and a young Charlie Chaplin, and also find time to indulge in a little kung-fu fighting.

programme.title: Lee Evans Live - Monsters
programme.desc: The legend of comedy returned in 2014 with his biggest show yet, with manic energy and uncanny observations, Lee Evans performing stand-up for a sell out crowd, one final time.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: `8 Out of 10 Cats is all pomp and ceremony as it celebrates the Queens Diamond Jubilee in its own inimitable style. As always Jimmy Carr hosts alongside regular team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, plus guests Chris Kamara and Jennie Bond.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: With Reginald D Hunter, Rachel Riley, Josh Widdicombe and Gemma Collins,.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Hollywood director Colin rejigs a casino robbery scene using demonic possession and `Les Miserables. Olympian Misty-May Treanor brings the muscle in a Worlds Strongest Women Competition.

programme.title: Dragony Aunts
programme.desc: Its time to embrace those bodies as the queens are helping comedian Stephen Bailey (Live at the Apollo, Celebs on the Farm, Celebrity Coach Trip) learn to love his chippy t***.

programme.title: Rust Valley Restorers

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: A contestant receives a NASCAR package presented by race car driver Jimmy Johnson, and must choose between a lifelong dream and a selfless act for his family.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: Gold Rushs Parker Schnabel joins Josh Gates to investigate an infamous California stagecoach robbery gone bad. Fighting a driving rainstorm, Josh leans on Parkers gold mining expertise to close in on the $10M lost treasure.

programme.title: Gold Divers
programme.desc: A new arrival shakes up the gold game as Emily shows Jane Kilcher the ropes of running a dredge; Kris Kelly deals with an excavator malfunction; Hurricane Dave is horrified by Vernons methods; digging deep pays off for Mr. Gold.

programme.title: Moonshiners
programme.desc: Moonshiners Mark, Digger, Tim, and Tickle delve deep into bourbon countrys darkest secret. When 200 bottles of the coveted Pappy Van Winkle vanish, the theft of the worlds most expensive bourbon is just the tip of a staggering criminal conspiracy.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: After receiving brand-new, frightening reports, Josh Gates sends Jessica and Phil to the woods of southern New Jersey to hunt for the infamous Jersey Devil. Locals are terrified by a demonic creature thats terrorised them for more than 300 years.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: When Josh Gates receives new, potential video proof of a giant creature living in Lake Champlain, he sends Phil and Jess to investigate the 30-foot serpent thats been spotted in the lake for generations.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Phil and Jessica uncover a rash of recent sightings and new evidence in the Pacific Northwest; new leads about a frightening encounter send them into rocky, dangerous terrain along Mount St. Helens.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Responding to recent UFO sightings, Josh Gates sends his team to New Mexico for a new investigation into the infamous, unsolved Socorro Landing Incident.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Josh Gates is on the ground with Jessica Chobot and Phil Torres to chase reports of a half-man, half-beast in rural Wisconsin.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Josh Gates, Jessica Chobot and Phil Torres are hot on the trail of a werewolf-like creature terrorising locals in Wisconsin. Eyewitness accounts and sightings lead them to a remote forest where frightening clues may reveal the beasts true identity.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.sub-title: Secret History of the New World

programme.title: Ed Staffords Rite of Passage

programme.title: Expedition Files

programme.title: Moonshiners
programme.desc: Mark and Digger recruit Patti Bryan to help make hot pepper moonshine and nearly blind themselves in the process. When Tickle over-orders blueberries for his restaurant, he has some explaining and shine-making to do.

programme.title: Moonshiners
programme.desc: Mark and Digger task Amanda and Kelly to find a poisonous berry they can turn into tasty, safe-to-drink shine. Tickle builds a `dead mans still to keep himself out of the doghouse. A bootleg river journey takes Mark and Huck into uncharted waters.

programme.title: Moonshiners
programme.desc: Mark and Digger recruit a young Kentucky moonshiner to help craft a profitable outlaw bourbon.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Locals Only
programme.desc: Pennsylvania locals race for $5000 with Caden Rankin, the son of an NPK racer, in the field, all bets are off. The winner faces Axman, known for never backing down.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Locals Only
programme.desc: Michiganders compete for $5,000, and a racing wagon surprises everyone. Then, the last local standing goes up against Bobby Ducote for another $5,000.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike and Edd decide that the 80s classic, Ford Mustang 5.0, is the ideal affordable muscle car for a young aspiring mechanic to cut his teeth on.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike and Edd decide to create an eye-catching, inexpensive beach cruiser, and the Corvair is the muse! Can the guys restore this classic to its original beauty?

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Edd and Mike set the challenge to find the ultimate bug-out vehicle that would ceaselessly run, and there was only one contender: the Land Rover Series IIA!

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys share memorable unseen moments from nearly a decade of rescuing homesteads, revealing how theyve enriched their lives and inspired them to keep fighting for homesteaders across the country.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: When neighbours send a plea for help, the Raneys rush to rescue a lone Montana homesteader. With winter bearing down upon them, they must build a survival strategy from scratch to secure shelter, water and food before its too late.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys struggle to revive an inherited Wyoming hill country homestead with a tragic legacy; to bring water to the property, Marty must solve the mystery of an untapped well; Misty and Matt combine forces on one of their most ambitious builds.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys meet their match on a homestead in Idaho, where millennial off-gridders are plagued with dead soil, grizzly bear visits and constant landslides. Making matters worse, COVID again strikes one of the Raneys, jeopardising the rescue.

programme.title: Moonshiners

programme.desc: Mark, Digger and Tim probe Brooklyns Civil War-era liquor tax battles; they examine how government use of Marines to enforce taxes led to clashes with urban moonshiners, establishing a pattern of conflict between tax collectors and distillers.

programme.desc: The team join the flood recovery efforts in their hometown of White Sulphur Springs by building a timber frame pavilion as the centrepiece of a memorial park.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: After receiving brand-new, frightening reports, Josh Gates sends Jessica and Phil to the woods of southern New Jersey to hunt for the infamous Jersey Devil. Locals are terrified by a demonic creature thats terrorised them for more than 300 years.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: When Josh Gates receives new, potential video proof of a giant creature living in Lake Champlain, he sends Phil and Jess to investigate the 30-foot serpent thats been spotted in the lake for generations.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Phil and Jessica uncover a rash of recent sightings and new evidence in the Pacific Northwest; new leads about a frightening encounter send them into rocky, dangerous terrain along Mount St. Helens.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Responding to recent UFO sightings, Josh Gates sends his team to New Mexico for a new investigation into the infamous, unsolved Socorro Landing Incident.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Josh Gates is on the ground with Jessica Chobot and Phil Torres to chase reports of a half-man, half-beast in rural Wisconsin.

programme.title: Expedition X
programme.desc: Josh Gates, Jessica Chobot and Phil Torres are hot on the trail of a werewolf-like creature terrorising locals in Wisconsin. Eyewitness accounts and sightings lead them to a remote forest where frightening clues may reveal the beasts true identity.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.desc: Mark and Digger task Amanda and Kelly to find a poisonous berry they can turn into tasty, safe-to-drink shine. Tickle builds a `dead mans still to keep himself out of the doghouse. A bootleg river journey takes Mark and Huck into uncharted waters.

programme.title: Kindig Customs

programme.title: Kindig Customs
programme.desc: Dave and the team start a one of a kind build they have never attempted before, a 1964 Ranchero. However, what is underneath the metal sets the team back.

programme.title: FourWheeler
programme.desc: Ian goes on an adventure with some of his buddies, hitting a few off-road parks nearby, making some roadside repairs, and ending the trip with some Tennessee whiskey.

programme.title: FourWheeler
programme.desc: Ian brings back the Suzuki Samurai, this time for an old-fashioned engine swap; hes going to take out the mechanical four-cylinder diesel VW engine and upgrade it with a common rail VW diesel.

programme.title: Bad Chad Customs
programme.desc: Chad builds a custom bare metal truck to give away at his Rockabilly car show. With the tightest of budgets, the team builds the truck from scratch, and Chads wild ideas keep them in overdrive.

programme.title: Sewer Divers
programme.desc: A mysterious clog backs up the entirety of the downtown neighbourhoods sewer line; the debris from a severe storm backs up a critical grit chamber; a restaurant grease trap is in dire need of cleaning.

programme.title: Sewer Divers
programme.desc: An abandoned sewer line is overwhelmed by stagnant human waste; the damage from a massive sinkhole threatens to shut down the entire citys sewer system; a large clog could take down a city building.

programme.title: Sewer Divers
programme.desc: Dirty Water Don repairs outflow gates at a treatment plant; Ramone and George discover medical waste lodged in a pump station; Rick deals with a disgusting grease trap at a meat cutting facility.

programme.title: Sydney Harbour Patrol
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes of the busiest transport and event hub in the world.

programme.title: Sydney Harbour Patrol
programme.desc: Police officer Gerard faces a huge public holiday party that threatens to spiral out of control. Deckhand Andy enters a danger zone to salvage a luxury boat from the ocean floor.

programme.title: Texas Metal
programme.desc: After John Cena makes a surprise appearance at the shop, Bill and the guys begin to customise Johns small 1969 MGC to be a perfect fit; a new customer wants a handmade lifted suspension on his 1920 Denali 2500 as a gift for his wife.

programme.title: Texas Metal
programme.desc: Bill and the team work their magic to customise a brand-new 2022 International wrecker; now that the pristine 1960s Willys Jeep has been quartered, they use imaginative fabrication techniques to make it whole again.

programme.title: Texas Metal
programme.desc: An Ekstensive favourite, a 2001 Hummer H1, needs some serious reinforcements; Bill changes things up on his completely custom, all-tube buggy by borrowing technology from monster trucks; Bill puts his daughter to work customising her 2020 Challenger.

programme.title: Garage Squad
programme.desc: A 1965 Chevy C10 was a father-son project until the father tragically lost his son. The truck has sat in the garage ever since, and now the Squad steps in to help.

programme.title: Garage Squad
programme.desc: A man neglected his car while supporting his family in the wake of a tragic loss. Now theyre back on their feet, the family want to show their appreciation.

programme.title: Garage Squad

programme.title: In Search of Monsters

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In West Yorkshire, there are terrible jokes, comedy teeth, plate-licking and a conga, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with Two Left Shoes
programme.desc: Stuart and Vicki are madly in love and desperate to get hitched, but cant find the funds. They are given £12,000 towards their big day, but the catch is that Stuart must organise the wedding alone. They will have no contact and Vicki wont know a single thing about her wedding until the big day three weeks later. Will music lover Stuart remember it is a wedding he is planning, not a concert?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Pool Party
programme.desc: Emma and Patrick are childhood sweethearts, but lack of funds means that Emma has been waiting nearly 10 years to get Patrick up the aisle. So, they are given £12,000 pounds towards their big day and a chance for Patrick to prove himself to the woman he loves. However, theres a catch - they have both agreed in the presence of a lawyer that Patrick must organise the wedding alone.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: James and Hollie are desperate to get hitched, but dont have the cash. They are given £12,000 and the chance for James to prove himself to the woman he loves by making every decision about the wedding alone. He and Hollie have agreed in the presence of a lawyer that she will know nothing until the big day. Will James find the perfect venue and get the dress right?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Drunk Bride
programme.desc: Nick and Laila are madly in love and have been engaged for two years. It would have taken them years to save up for Lailas dream wedding day, instead they will receive £12,000 towards their big day and a chance for Nick to prove himself to the woman he loves. They have agreed that Nick will organise the wedding alone and the bride wont know a single thing about it until the big day.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Tony and Pippa are desperate to get hitched, but dont have the cash. They are given £12,000 and the chance for Tony to prove himself to the woman he loves by making every decision about the wedding. He and Pippa have agreed in the presence of a lawyer that she will know absolutely nothing until the big day. Three weeks apart will push their relationship to the limit. Will his best men give him the best advice?

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Wilkinsburg, Pennsylvania, home to Ms Jeans Southern Cuisine, which was opened 12 years ago by retired special education teacher Ms Jean Gould. Her employees say shes like Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde and thats why the local community stay away. Ms Jean insists shes only mean because her employees are lazy, but the business is falling apart.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across the United States in a bid to save failing restaurants from disaster. This week, Gordon visits beachside restaurant Barefoot Bobs in Hull, Massachusetts, owned by husband-and-wife team Marc and Lisa. The duo opened the grill in the summer of 2004 to a busy seafront crowd, but every year the bottom line collapses through the bleak winter months.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits Cafe Hon in Hampden, Maryland, Chiarellas in Philadelphia, and Leones in Montclair, New Jersey, to see whether the restaurant owners have taken his advice on board.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon head to the historic Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, where he meets an owner so stubborn and set in his ways that he doesnt realise hes the reason his restaurant is losing money. Owner Tom bought the business for his daughter Andrea, but six years later he is still refusing to let her run it. Gordon must fight a controlling boss who doesnt react well to his criticism.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In Beaver, Pennsylvania, where he finds a brother-and-sister team who are unable to work together in a positive manner at an Italian restaurant called Levantis. Their relationship is so fractured that they are failing to save their fathers business from being overwhelmed by competition from other local eateries. The situation has become desperate and dad Tony is now in danger of losing his life savings.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a the hit comedy show, packed with silly games and surprises. Team captains Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan take on Jon Richardson and David Mitchell. Nick Helm is in Dictionary corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley is back on numbers-and-letters duty.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Henning Wehn go up against Jon Richardson and Victoria Coren Mitchell. Tom Allen joins Countdowns resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.desc: Childhood sweethearts Rodney and Paula are about to get wed but are strapped for cash. Can Rodney find the perfect venue, will he ever make a decision or will making his mind up tip his bride over the edge?

programme.sub-title: The One with the Wedding Casino

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In West Yorkshire, Sophie and Harj plan a Mexican menu, if Harj can find his way around the kitchen. Richard and Libby stick to British classics. Butcher Sean and Jane serve a meat feast.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: High-flyers Bob and Louise, ditzy Will and Kim, and Iranian expats Pasha and Haleh compete in Cheltenham, in a week featuring massive portions, a tale of heroism and Persian rice pudding.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, Debbie and Paul, Angela and John, and Kirsty and James compete in a week featuring a malfunctioning blender, a singing dog and plenty of catty comments.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gill and Mark, Matt and Derek, and Cat and Martin compete to host the best dinner party in Glasgow, in a week featuring an impromptu sing-song, a kitchen disaster and an MC battle.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits family-owned restaurant Sams Mediterranean Kabob Room in Monrovia, California. Within minutes of his arrival, Gordon is caught in the crossfire of an argument between owner Sam, his wife and their children, all of whom are feeling the pressure of working in the restaurant seven days a week.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Long Beach, California, to save Ninos, an Italian family restaurant that was opened in 1958 and is now under the misguided leadership of the eldest son, Nino. Younger brother Michael and sister Carina point the finger of blame for the restaurants failings directly at Nino, and their 78-year-old mother Inge is desperate for Nino to turn the business around.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Norwalk, Ohio, in Americas farming heartland, for the first of two visits to the Mill Street Bistro. There he encounters Joe Nagy, one of the most pretentious restaurant owners he has ever met.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the dramatic conclusion to the Mill Street Bistro saga, Gordons disagreement with proprietor Joe Nagy erupts into one of his most intense confrontations yet.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Greek restaurant Yannis in Seattle, where he clashes with defiant owner Peter, a chef who is so set in his ways that he hasnt allowed any changes to his menu since the business opened in 1984. His own family are concerned that the restaurant is becoming trapped in the past, but Peter is determined to stick to his guns.

programme.title: Happy Death Day 2U
programme.desc: College student Tree Gelbman becomes the target of a masked killer when she wakes up in an alternate reality. When the psychopath starts to go after her inner circle, Tree soon realizes that she must die over and over again to save her friends.

programme.desc: Ben is a teacher who meets high-powered business woman Lindsey Meeks. They hit it off and their feelings grow for each other until she realises theres someone else in his life - the Boston Red Sox baseball team.

programme.title: The Call of the Wild
programme.desc: In the Canadian Yukon in the 1890s, the gold rush is happening. At this time, the sled dogs who can cover the tough terrain along the river Klondike are coveted by many and they become a valuable asset. As a result, Buck is stolen and sold.

programme.title: Mrs Doubtfire
programme.desc: Slapstick film about a divorced actor who is willing to go to any lengths to get his children back, even posing as a matronly Scottish housekeeper. His family, taken in by the disguise, decide to employ the eccentric but apparently capable widow, and everyone is happy with the arrangement - until the mothers old boyfriend reappears and tries to become a father figure to the children.

programme.desc: Jerry Selbee discovers a mathematical loophole in the Massachusetts lottery, and with the help of his wife, Marge, he wins millions of dollars.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieris hitting the road and grabbing grub straight out of Mums kitchen. In Boulder, a mother-son team servin up Chinese specialties like dan dan noodles and steamed buns.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri is diggin into dishes featuring some dynamite dough. In Mesa, a legit Hawaiian joint serving island specialties, including a meaty bun, and in Tucson, a place cookin up a big-time saucy sandwich.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Scrumptious cooking series with Ina Garten. Ina cooks with a classic ingredient, chicken. Her chicken salad and rotisserie chicken satay are real corkers.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: The delightful Ina Garten reveals her cooking tips. Ina cooks farm fresh food including scalloped tomatoes and crisp salad, and she hosts her very own question and answer session.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: The delightful Ina Garten reveals her cooking tips. Ina prepares a delicious grilled feast of Indonesian swordfish and later, a chef Joe Realmuto concocts a punchy BBQ sauce.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Scrumptious cooking series with Ina Garten. Baker Kathleen King shows Ina how to make mouthwatering double chocolate almond cookies, and her Irish soda bread is easy to prepare.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: The delightful Ina Garten reveals her cooking tips. Featuring recipes for a delicious rum and raisin tiramisu, and a gorgeous no cook Italian anti pasti platter.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: The delightful Ina Garten reveals her cooking tips. Ina makes lasagne with a Greek twist, and an arugula and watermelon feta salad.

programme.title: Nadiyas Fast Flavours
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain conjures up stress-free recipes that are ideal to chill out with on a lazy day at home. She begins with banana bread, the recipe that became a lockdown cliche.

programme.title: Nadiyas Fast Flavours
programme.desc: Nadiya turns her attention to one of her favourite things, special occasions. She starts with her lemon drizzle swirls. A twist on the cinnamon bun, these fluffy lemon- and cardamom-scented creations are topped with a pillowy citrus frosting.

programme.title: Nadiyas Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya shares her favourite time-saving recipes to take the stress out of impressing guests. Recipes include raspberry cheesecake croissants, a satay chicken tray bake, and a cheats trifle with chia seeds, yoghurt, fresh fruit and sweet brioche.

programme.title: Nadiyas Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya shares her favourite recipes for when she wants to whip up something sweet and delicious, but deceptively simple. Recipes include milkshake funnel cakes, a giant cookie, and a nutty baklava. On the road, Nadiya joins the team at Tate and Lyle on the River Thames.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: Continuing his travels around France, James visits a region known for its traditional hearty cuisine, buried treasures and a love of all things duck - the Dordogne.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: James Martins travels around France continue in the city of Lyon - known as the gastronomic capital of France - where he explores the two sides of the culinary scene.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary is off to explore how different families use food to create their own wonderful sense of home. Mary meets a father-and-daughter team who have taken their home cooking to the streets with their own food van.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary heads to the dramatic Yorkshire coastline to celebrate a unique bonfire party, explore the ancient seaport of Whitby and sample some classic, coastal comfort food, fish and chips and ice cream. Mary visits a small smokehouse that has been run by the same family for five generations, where she helps to smoke herring to make their world-renowned kippers.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary makes delicious food to warm her up after a day out in the cold, including a tempting sausage and pepper hotpot, some simple twice-baked souffles, a mushroom, lentil and double potato jumble and an onion and sage open puff pastry tart.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cook and Share
programme.desc: Mary is working behind the scenes at an Indian wedding, shell be helping the caterers pull off this huge event that feeds 400 people from morning to night while adding a recipe of her own to the menu.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cook and Share
programme.desc: Mary travels to the beautiful city of Bristol. Her connection to the city is extensive: it was here that she had her first job and where her son William studied at university, and its currently home to her granddaughter Grace.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cook and Share
programme.desc: Mary is off to Birmingham, home of the world-famous cricket ground at Edgbaston, where she immerses herself in the world of cricket.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cook and Share
programme.desc: Mary explores the historic Jurassic Coast, famous for fossils, beautiful coastal towns and delicious seafood. Mary cant wait to share the delights on offer and meet the people passionate about preserving and showcasing them.

programme.title: Secret Eats With Adam Richman
programme.desc: Adam seeks out Singapores secret eats, including chilli crab at a hard-to-find restaurant, chasiu at a speakeasy hidden beneath a ramen shop, and a gourmet hot dog with candied bacon at an eatery in a movie theatre parking garage.

programme.title: Secret Eats With Adam Richman
programme.desc: Adam eats like a local in Bangkok, where he finds hidden restaurants serving spicy curries, traditional boat noodles served in a locals-only floating market and an off-menu fusion Pad Thai at one of the best noodle shops.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieris diving into all kinds of comfort cooking. In Phoenix, a funky joint stackin up barbecue fries and a new spin on chilaquiles. In Boulder, a pizza place loading up the pies and their meatball grinder.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri dives into delicious dishes on both coasts. In Palo Alto, a funky joint puts out fresh California cooking, like spicy pork and knockout gnocchi. In Wilmington, a bakery-restaurant makes major meatloaf.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieris digging into a multicultural flavourfest. In Hawaii, a poke place knocking it out of the park with their island specialties. In San Carlos, a crazy Italian spot putting tasty twists on their homemade pasta.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri grabs some off-the-hook seafood and savoury sensations. In Lansing, a food truck dishes out shrimp burgers and a unique potato popper. In Tucson, a Bosnian restaurant fires up legit Eastern European specialities.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri looks for culinary creations you can really sink your teeth into, including a New Hampshire catering truck full of beef tips, shrimp and dirty bird wings. Then, theres rockin pierogi in Ohio and homemade gnocchi at a New Jersey eatery.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.sub-title: Triple D Nation: Soups, Stews and Seafood

programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri uncovers some surprising culinary discoveries. In Columbus, Ohio, a hidden gem tucked away in a grocery store serves out-of-bounds Nepalese-Tibetan specialties.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris crisscrossing the nation to grab some international grub. In Dallas, a funky Thai joint serving up noodles, curry and green papaya salad. In Miami, a Latin place dealing in empanadas and a unique plantain sandwich.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Springfield, Illinois, Adam takes on one of Americas oldest eating challenges - the Firebrand Chili Challenge.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman eats his way around America. In Boise, Idaho, his first stop is Big Juds, home of the massive Double Big Jud, a burger featuring two Frisbee-sized patties.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman continues his quest to find the best eats in the USA. Baltimore Maryland boasts a chowdown must: flame grilled beef sliced thin and piled high.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richmans quest for Americas best eats takes him to Michigan. He gathers a team of Detroit natives to help him attack a two-and-a-half-foot tall, 190-pound burger.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richmans search for Americas best grub takes him to Tucson, Arizona. The belt busting challenges include an enormous combo platter called `The Presidents Plate.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Another belt-busting challenge for Adam Richman. Here, he takes a cross-country journey through three minor league baseball stadiums known for their grub.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: More belt-busting adventures for Adam Richman. His hunger quest takes him to Little Rock, Arkansas where the eating challenges include a fiery pulled pork sandwich.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: More belt-busting adventures for Adam Richman. In Washington DC, can he devour a 116-ounce milkshake and a one and a half pound sandwich in just one hour?

programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: James travels to the lakeside town of Annecy, where he cooks dishes showcasing local tomatoes, red mullet and lobster, before visiting award-winning pastry chef Philippe Rigollot.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: Heading east, James travels to the region of Jura. Local famer Matthew Tissot shows James around his orchard and teaches him about the regions remarkable apples and pears.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: This time the judges set challenges inspired by the roaring twenties, including a slapstick Signature and a tiered Showstopper offering a twist on their favourite tipple.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The cake layers stack up in an elegant and challenging bake for Dessert Week. Theres also a spherical celebration cake and a Technical where success lies in precise decoration.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Festival Week as the bakers draw inspiration from around the world, including something deep fried and Italian, and a spectacular Indonesian celebration cake.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers have their knowledge of pastry tested to the max with a savoury take on a French classic, a multi-layered Moroccan Technical, and a towering Showstopper.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: On the next leg of his UK tour, James is joined in glorious Devon by his friend and Michelin-starred chef Simon Hulstone. James visits an extraordinary mushroom farm situated in a bungalow, and makes a tasty steak and mushroom dish. Simon samples some award-winning cheese, which inspires James to make a cheese platter fit for a king. The day ends with a trip to the Art Deco splendour of Burgh Island.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by pal and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, and together they set out to explore the delights of Jersey aboard mini motor bikes. James samples the delights of one of Jerseys most famous exports, the Jersey Royal potato, and he uses them to cook up a squid risotto with a difference. Mark Jordan gets a lesson in oyster shucking, and James discovers an island speciality: Jersey black butter, which he uses to create a delicious sweet treat.
